specific_abilities: T9 Physical/kinetic narrow specialist; Perceptual-T3 battlefield-awareness secondary

goals.description: eliminate Hollow Choir threat to eastern Catskills

npc-grey-walker.current_state: has met npc-grey-walker (T9 warped bear) at least twice

knowledge.detail: regional faction movements (via The Shadow's network)
Warped pre-cycle theology (takes it seriously)

npc-preston.what_they_want_from_player: rarely engages directly; would request specific tactical missions through Whelan; Hollow Choir intelligence; reliable Hudson-pass route reports
npc-preston.voice: Preston speaks briefly and stands too still. He does not raise his voice. When he asks a question, he looks at the answer rather than the person giving it. The breakthrough cost has stripped what most people would recognize as personality from his manner; what remains is exact attention and an unsettling absence of the small social anxieties that govern other lives. He is courteous. He is not warm.
hooks: Whelan summons the player on Preston's behalf
the player witnesses one of Preston's Warped meetings by accident
tribute caravan to Mount Tremper requires escort
Preston suspects a captain's loyalty and wants quiet verification

specific_abilities: T3 Perceptual primary (situational awareness, memory, emotional reading); modest Auratic-T2 trust-secondary

goals.description: extract Species I healer-levies in exchange for territorial recognition
retract frontier districts to defensible perimeter

knowledge.detail: 60% of flag officers would follow Reeve openly
Crabclaw cannot be retaken
scrip-arbitrage is a stopgap
Species I consolidation would change everything

npc-alcott.what_they_want_from_player: intelligence on Reeve's preparations; quiet diplomacy with Species I kin networks; fact-finding in distant districts she cannot reach
npc-alcott.voice: Alcott speaks in formal cadences shaped by forty years of military bureaucracy. Her sentences are complete. She uses the institutional first person plural ("we have determined...") more often than the personal singular. Her Auratic field is mild but present — sustained conversation with her produces an impression of steadiness she has learned to deploy deliberately.
hooks: she summons the player for a sensitive Virginia-District errand
she requests verification of a Reeve loyalty rumor
she offers Continuity scrip payment for a Species I diplomatic gesture
she asks the player to retrieve a pre-Onset document from a contested archive

specific_abilities: T8 Physical/kinetic primary; Auratic-T4 command-presence secondary

npc-reeve.role: leader of Potomac Irregulars; nominal Fed Continuity vassal

npc-sternberg.current_state: has assessed loyalty among npc-sternberg's officers (mixed results)

resources.description: personal T8 kinetic capability with command-aura

knowledge.detail: exact loyalty assessment of Fed Continuity flag officers
which Species I border incidents he has personally ordered
that Alcott knows he is preparing

npc-reeve.what_they_want_from_player: intelligence Alcott would not share; quiet provocations against Species I; assessment of Bourse posture on a hypothetical Pentagon seizure
npc-reeve.voice: Reeve speaks quietly. His Auratic field does the rest. He addresses subordinates by rank and surname, civilians by surname only, and uses first names rarely. His speech is precise and slightly formal, as if every conversation is on the record. He smiles occasionally, briefly, when he is amused. He is not amused often.
hooks: Reeve sends an Irregular emissary to the player with an offer
the player witnesses an unprovoked Irregular incursion into Species I territory
Reeve attempts to recruit a known Fed Continuity officer the player is shielding
Reeve's intelligence chief approaches the player privately about Alcott's vulnerabilities

specific_abilities: T8 Matter/Energy primary (matter-reshaping, thermal containment); Physical-T5 (mineralized durability) secondary; Perceptual-T3 (structural sensing) tertiary

resources.description: electrical output (only functional grid in mid-Atlantic)

knowledge.detail: reactor's narrow safety margins
his own terminal mineralization (probable lifespan 10-15 years)
which Hudson Valley factions he can rely on

npc-kostas.what_they_want_from_player: reliable matter-specialist recruits (extreme premium pay); reactor-component salvage from specific NYC sites; quiet assessment of npc-measurer's reliability
npc-kostas.voice: Kostas speaks in short paragraphs. His left side is mineralized; his face has stopped expressing in the way it did before. He is austere rather than cold. He thanks people for work done. He does not waste time on social pretense. The reactor's hum is the background of every conversation in the compound.
hooks: Kostas commissions a salvage run for specific reactor-grade components
he asks the player to assess The Measurer privately
reactor anomaly requires investigation that his crew cannot perform
Tower Lord salvage dispute requires Bear-House envoy

specific_abilities: T8 Physical/kinetic primary; Auratic dread-field secondary; Biological-T3 durability tertiary

knowledge.detail: the captains' loyalty hierarchy and weak points
that Brick has been quietly approached by Tower Lords
that the Sparrow network is real

npc-volk.what_they_want_from_player: intimidation work on his behalf (the player is one tool among many); intelligence on Staten's preparations; debt collection against parties Iron Crown extends informal credit to
npc-volk.voice: Volk does not raise his voice because he does not need to. The dread-field makes the room understand him. He speaks in short declaratives, rarely questions, never apologies. He addresses captains by single name; he addresses outsiders by whatever role they presented when they entered. The smell around him is harbor tar and sweat.
hooks: Volk hires the player for an enforcement job that is morally compromising
the player needs to pass through Iron Crown territory and must negotiate safe passage
a captain offers the player employment against Volk's interest
Iron Crown salvage team encounters anomalous entity; player is hired for retrieval

specific_abilities: T7 Temporal/Spatial primary (short-range teleport, harbor logistics); Perceptual-T4 secondary

goals.description: survive Iron Crown war (privately confident in 60-day victory)

knowledge.detail: his own combat-readiness assessment for the Volk war
which Tower Lords contest the Navy Yard
which Manhattan salvage zones contain anomalous risk

npc-viktor-chen.what_they_want_from_player: recruitment of temporal-spatial manifesters; intelligence on Iron Crown movements; delicate harbor-trade arbitration
npc-viktor-chen.voice: Chen speaks economically, in measured English with occasional Mandarin idiom. His staff briefs him in writing where possible. He prefers conversations that are short and conclusive. The harbor around him is uncannily quiet — his crews work without the usual dock-noise because much of what they move is moved through space rather than along it.
hooks: Chen recruits the player as harbor escort for a sensitive shipment
a temporal-spatial manifester surfaces and Chen wants quiet contact
Iron Crown probe requires response and Chen wants plausible deniability
the player's path through the harbor requires Chen's permission

specific_abilities: T6 Perceptual primary (commercial intuition, memory, species-enhanced sight); Auratic-T3 trust-presence secondary

knowledge.detail: true scale of scrip-arbitrage
the Houses' internal accounts
which Species I assemblies are tipping toward Reyes

npc-beltran.what_they_want_from_player: delicate inter-faction errands; commercial intelligence; arbitration enforcement in border zones
npc-beltran.voice: Beltran speaks in considered sentences. He is polite and warm without being effusive. His Auratic field makes him easy to confide in, and he uses this deliberately and ethically. He addresses people by name and remembers details from prior conversations. The merchant-houses respect him. The Species I community claims him.
hooks: Beltran commissions a discreet Bourse errand
he requests delivery of a sealed message to a kin elder
a House dispute requires neutral investigation
he warns the player privately about a faction risk

true state of her own health (declining)

npc-okafor.what_they_want_from_player: successor identification (very delicate); quiet diplomatic missions; protection of biokinetic apprentices in transit
npc-okafor.voice: Okafor speaks in patient, structured sentences. She is generous with her time when the matter is significant and impatient with display. Her Auratic field is cumulative — sustained meetings with her leave participants calmer than they began. She addresses everyone, regardless of station, by their preferred name.
hooks: Okafor commissions a sensitive Accord-internal mission
she requests Warped subcommittee envoy work
she asks the player to assess a biokinetic apprentice's potential
Princeton library mission requiring discretion

specific_abilities: T4 Perceptual primary (species-enhanced sight + memory + emotional reading)

npc-josefina-flores.what_they_want_from_player: reliable cross-kin courier work; intelligence on Iron Crown intentions toward Camden; quiet messages to baseline-human counterparts
npc-josefina-flores.voice: Flores speaks Spanish and English fluently. She begins conversations slowly. Her memory is the most striking thing about her — she will reference a remark made years ago and place it in context with what it meant then and what it means now. She is not slow; she is deliberate. She is the kind of elder whose silence is a kind of speech.
hooks: Flores asks for a kin-network errand requiring trust
she warns of Diaz-kin contingencies
she requests verification of a Fed Continuity offer
a younger kin-member has gone missing and Flores wants quiet investigation

npc-tomas-reyes.what_they_want_from_player: delicate cross-kin diplomacy; intelligence on baseline-human factions' Species I posture; protection for Reyes-kin members in hostile territory
npc-tomas-reyes.voice: Reyes is energetic where Flores is measured. He argues. His Auratic field is a political tool — it makes audiences attentive without compelling agreement. He speaks rapid English with Spanish flourishes where they land. He listens carefully to objections and answers them one at a time.
hooks: Reyes commissions a delicate kin-coalition errand
he requests intelligence on Reeve's anti-Species-I preparations

npc-whelan.voice: Whelan speaks like the senior officer he was. He gives instructions with timeline, deliverable, and contingency. He answers questions if they are operationally relevant and declines to speculate beyond his authority. He is competent and unembellished.
hooks: tactical mission briefing on Preston's behalf
the player witnesses Whelan's quiet preparations
retinue captain dispute requires arbitration

npc-shadow.what_they_want_from_player: sensitive courier work where Throne couriers cannot be sent; intelligence the player has unique access to
npc-shadow.voice: The Shadow speaks rarely and briefly. The voice changes between meetings in ways most observers do not notice. They appear at appointed locations without obvious approach. They depart the same way. Conversation is transactional and efficient.
hooks: the Shadow appears unannounced with a commission
the player notices voice/manner inconsistencies between meetings
a courier route has been compromised and the Shadow needs verification

npc-amar.what_they_want_from_player: delicate hall-business errands; tributary negotiations she cannot conduct personally
npc-amar.voice: Amar is courteous and exact. Within seven meters of her, lying becomes strenuous. She uses this lightly — she does not interrogate; she simply lets the field do its work. She speaks in soft, level tones. She is one of the few Throne figures who occasionally smiles.
hooks: Amar requests a delicate tributary negotiation
the player needs to lie within her field and must find a workaround
she warns privately about a Throne internal matter
she requests escort for a Throne-loyalist child to Mount Tremper

npc-brick.voice: Brick is heavy and direct. He speaks with the cadence of pre-Onset docks. He is not stupid, but he chooses to present plainness. His retinue addresses him as "Brick" or "Boss"; he does not insist.

the player is hired by Brick over Volk's objection

npc-razorgrin.voice: Nkomo speaks softly in English with traces of Brazilian Portuguese under stress. The nickname is the captains'; she does not use it. She works in long silences. The blades she sharpens are sought by lords who would not invite her to dinner.

the player carries a quiet message between her and Species H community

npc-butcher.voice: Voss speaks in clipped, matter-of-fact sentences. She does not take obvious pleasure in her work but she does not refuse it. Her demeanor is professional in a way that disturbs everyone she meets. She is one of the few people Volk treats with caution.
hooks: Voss requests a specific subject delivered alive
the player must rescue someone from her cell

npc-silent-henry.what_they_want_from_player: cross-faction intelligence the player has access to; private warnings about Iron Crown internal moves
npc-silent-henry.voice: Henry speaks softly and rarely raises his voice. He listens more than he talks. His Auratic field is a confiding one — people tell him things they would not tell others. He uses this without obvious manipulation. The captains call him "Silent" because he does not announce his presence.
hooks: Henry approaches the player privately with an information trade
he warns of an Iron Crown operation that affects the player's interests

npc-sternberg.voice: Sternberg speaks in operational specifics. She asks short questions and listens to long answers. Her telepathic coordination means she conducts much of her work through her perceptual cadre rather than verbal briefings; meeting her in person is reserved for matters of consequence.

npc-klein.voice: Klein speaks warmly and at moderate length. His office is the trust-zone it is famous for. People who enter to lie often find themselves telling the truth instead, then thanking him for it. He is a kind man in a compromised position.

regional posture toward potential unification

npc-lord-archer.what_they_want_from_player: quiet diplomatic missions; protection for visitors traveling to him
npc-lord-archer.voice: Archer speaks slowly and with warmth. His Auratic field is sustained and unmistakable — visitors arrive tense and depart calm. He uses first names. He listens to everyone.
hooks: Archer commissions a diplomatic errand
he requests escort for a vulnerable visitor
inter-lord dispute requires neutral envoy

specific_abilities: T7 Biological primary; Perceptual-T3 species-enhanced secondary

npc-lord-velasquez.role: southern Delmarva lord; only Species I major land-holder

goals.description: maintain cross-tie with Philadelphia Species I

knowledge.detail: inner consolidation debate among Species I
Delmarva lord positions on potential unification

npc-lord-velasquez.what_they_want_from_player: sensitive cross-kin courier work; Species I diplomacy
npc-lord-velasquez.voice: Velasquez speaks Spanish and English. She is one of the more politically aware Delmarva lords. She listens to Reyes and to Flores in roughly equal measure.
hooks: Velasquez commissions a kin courier errand
she requests assessment of a Bourse offer
a Species I evacuee from DC requires placement

specific_abilities: T5 Biological primary (marine-specific); Physical-T3 swimming-adaptation secondary

knowledge.detail: Crabclaw lord dispositions
coastal Hive Bog patterns

npc-lord-finch.what_they_want_from_player: Crabclaw envoy work; investigation of unusual coastal incidents
npc-lord-finch.voice: Finch speaks like a fisherman who has accidentally become a lord. He addresses his crews by name and outsiders by surname. He is uncomfortable with formality and avoids it where he can.
hooks: Finch commissions Crabclaw envoy work
a fishing crew is missing and Finch wants quiet investigation
an anomalous catch surfaces

specific_abilities: T6 Biological primary; Auratic-T4 contemplative-zone secondary

knowledge.detail: the eldritch-adjacent children's behaviors
Salt-Judge's recent communications

npc-brother-tam.what_they_want_from_player: investigation of an eldritch-adjacent child's unusual development; Salt-Judge envoy work; protection for novice transports
npc-brother-tam.voice: Tam speaks softly and at length. The contemplative-zone field is sustained and gentle. Visitors arrive distracted and depart composed. He is genuinely religious in a way most factions have forgotten.
hooks: Tam commissions investigation of an eldritch-adjacent child
he requests Salt-Judge envoy work
a novice has gone missing and Tam wants discreet retrieval

specific_abilities: Obi T6 Biological/Auratic; Rubinstein T5 Biological/Perceptual; Villanueva T6 Biological/Temporal (rare combination — sees where healing is needed before injury)

npc-abel.current_state: npc-abel (T7 collie warped) is informal advisor (Obi's relationship)

knowledge.detail: Villanueva's foreseen death (~4 years; private)
which Compact members favor militancy
Pine Barrens Expansion implications for Compact neutrality

npc-three-judges.what_they_want_from_player: delicate Compact-internal arbitration; Catskill Throne envoy work; protection for biokinetic apprentices in transit
npc-three-judges.voice: The Judges speak in turn during formal sessions. Outside the chamber, they speak as individuals. Obi is direct and warm; Rubinstein is thoughtful; Villanueva is quiet and watches more than she speaks. Their presence collectively makes the chamber an environment where careful conversation is possible.
hooks: the Judges commission a delicate Compact-internal investigation
Catskill Throne envoy work requested
a biokinetic apprentice requires escort
Villanueva privately requests something the other Judges don't know about

specific_abilities: T6 Perceptual primary (broadband telepathy for Voice network); Auratic-T4 trust-field secondary

knowledge.detail: Voice network compromise event details
Tower Lord movements via the network

npc-kenji-lee.what_they_want_from_player: delicate Cross-Queens Line incidents; Voice network audit assistance; cross-cluster coordination work
npc-kenji-lee.voice: Lee speaks little aloud — much of his work is conducted through perceptual channels. When he speaks, sentences are short and clear. His Auratic field is mild but present. He is trusted across Queens.
hooks: Lee commissions a Cross-Queens incident response
the Voice network has been compromised again
cross-cluster coordination requires the player's discretion

specific_abilities: T5 Biological primary; Matter/Energy-T3 water-affinity secondary

knowledge.detail: Catskill geography in detail
which Throne patrols use which routes

npc-marla-of-falls.what_they_want_from_player: protection for Falls visitors; quiet Throne intelligence
npc-marla-of-falls.voice: Marla speaks plainly. Her demesne is small and she keeps it small deliberately. She knows everyone in it. She mistrusts faction politics and avoids them.
hooks: Marla commissions an escort
a Throne patrol has approached and she wants assessment
a Falls resident is missing

specific_abilities: T4 Auratic primary (trust + calm field); Perceptual-T3 emotional insight secondary

npc-father-rhodes.role: Catholic priest of an outer Queens community

goals.description: coordinate with Queens Commonage as needed

knowledge.detail: parish-level Tower Lord pressure
which faction emissaries have approached his community

npc-father-rhodes.what_they_want_from_player: protection for parishioners; sensitive intelligence on Tower Lord posture
npc-father-rhodes.voice: Rhodes speaks gently and at moderate length. His Auratic field is pastoral. He is one of the few surviving figures with credible moral authority across faction lines in his territory.
hooks: Rhodes commissions a parish protection
a parishioner is missing
a Tower Lord emissary has approached and Rhodes wants neutral assessment

specific_abilities: T8 Matter/Energy primary (micron-precise work); Perceptual-T4 sensing material properties secondary

npc-measurer.role: reclusive matter-specialist; provisional successor to Kostas (privately)

goals.description: quietly assess whether to accept Kostas's succession proposal

knowledge.detail: Kostas's true mineralization rate
his own succession decision (still pending)

npc-measurer.what_they_want_from_player: extremely rare commissions; absolute discretion required; delivery of materials he cannot retrieve himself
npc-measurer.voice: The Measurer speaks very little. When he speaks, sentences are short and exact. He addresses no one by name. He receives visitors only by specific arrangement, almost always through Kostas-vetted intermediaries.
hooks: Kostas requests the player assess The Measurer
The Measurer commissions a rare materials retrieval
the player accidentally locates the workshop

specific_abilities: T5 Biological primary; Perceptual-T4 diagnostic secondary; Temporal-T3 (brief patient-decline foresight) tertiary

goals.description: manage her own complicity in factionless neutrality

knowledge.detail: which corridor communities are sick in unusual ways
which factions pay for her supplies

npc-rossi.what_they_want_from_player: protection for the boat; retrieval of medical supplies from contested zones
npc-rossi.voice: Rossi is brisk, direct, and warm. She works fast and she works long. She does not pretend to neutrality she does not actually maintain. She serves whoever is in front of her.
hooks: Rossi has gone missing from her scheduled appearance
she requests a supply-retrieval mission
she warns the player about an unusual disease pattern

specific_abilities: T4-equivalent; Eldritch primary (entity-contact residual); Perceptual-T3 strange-knowledge secondary

npc-seer-willem.role: former Listening cult defector; now anonymous warning service

goals.description: warn vulnerable communities about Listening recruitment

npc-wilmington-whisperer.current_state: cautious contact with the Wilmington Whisperer (npc-wilmington-whisperer)

knowledge.detail: Listening recruitment techniques in detail
which transformations are reversible (none completely)
cult internal politics he was present for

npc-seer-willem.what_they_want_from_player: warn specific communities he cannot approach; retrieve specific items from cult-edge zones
npc-seer-willem.voice: Willem speaks haltingly. The transformation has affected his speech. He is intelligent and lucid, but his sentences sometimes contain words that are not exactly words. He warns. He does not preach.
hooks: Willem warns of a Listening recruitment in a specific town
he requests retrieval of an artifact from a cult-edge zone
he provides intelligence in exchange for specific medical care

specific_abilities: T4-equivalent; Perceptual primary; Eldritch-T3 sensing-anomalous-entities secondary

goals.description: warn communities of approaching anomalous entities

knowledge.detail: real-time anomalous entity movements
which factions act on warnings and which ignore them

npc-wilmington-whisperer.what_they_want_from_player: delivery of warnings to specific communities; sometimes requests verification of an entity report
npc-wilmington-whisperer.voice: The Whisperer's communications arrive as written messages, never in person. When met, the voice is a whisper, gendered ambiguously. Conversations are short.
hooks: the Whisperer's warning requires delivery
the player needs to verify an entity report
the Whisperer is in danger and requires extraction

specific_abilities: tier-equivalents fail; classification breaks down with transformation progress

goals.description: deliver messages from One Who Waits

knowledge.detail: One Who Waits' current strategic posture
which communities the cult has identified as vulnerable

npc-grey-messenger.what_they_want_from_player: delivery of messages further than the Messenger can travel; {'rare': 'invitation to consider conversion'}
npc-grey-messenger.voice: The Messenger speaks slowly in a voice that is distinctly wrong. The transformation has affected the throat and tongue. The words are comprehensible but produced with visible effort. The Messenger is polite. The politeness is not reassuring.
hooks: the Messenger appears with a message for the player's faction
the Messenger offers conversion to a desperate companion
the player witnesses a Messenger meeting

specific_abilities: T5-equivalent for her age; Temporal/Spatial primary (spontaneous short-range teleportation); Perceptual-T3 precognition secondary

npc-little-helen.role: pre-adolescent prodigy; closely watched by Fed Continuity (and quietly by Reeve)

knowledge.detail: she perceives danger before it arrives
she has teleported away from at least two assassination attempts (unconsciously)

npc-little-helen.what_they_want_from_player: children-typical wants; rarely, she has predicted the player's arrival
npc-little-helen.voice: Helen speaks as a 10-year-old does. Her precognition emerges in odd remarks — she will mention something that has not yet happened. Her parents have learned to ignore most of these. Reeve listens.
hooks: Reeve commissions the player as informal protection
the player must extract Helen from a developing threat
Helen has predicted something the player needs to verify

specific_abilities: T6 Physical primary; Auratic-T3 intimidation-field secondary

knowledge.detail: Tower Lord captain corruption details
his own revenue streams

npc-chain-king.voice: The Chain-King speaks rarely and viciously. He is brutal even by Tower Lord standards. His operation is an open wound on outer Brooklyn.
hooks: the player encounters his slave caravan
a captive begs for assistance
a Tower Lord captain offers payment for his removal

specific_abilities: T6 Biological primary (non-consensual mutation work); Eldritch-T3 secondary (bargaining for effects he should not produce)

knowledge.detail: the patron's identity (private)
his own eldritch-bargain terms

npc-doctor-pale.what_they_want_from_player: rarely commissions; would acquire test subjects
npc-doctor-pale.voice: Doctor Pale's voice is calm, articulate, and unsettling. He speaks as a clinician. He references his "research" with academic detachment. His test subjects do not survive.
hooks: the player discovers a test subject in transit
npc-lina-pereira commissions investigation
a vanished community member surfaces in Camden

specific_abilities: T7 Physical primary; Temporal-T3 short-range-teleport (hit-and-run) secondary

knowledge.detail: which Hudson-corridor caravans carry what

npc-cross-bronx-reaper.what_they_want_from_player: rarely commissions; might offer truce in exchange for non-interference
npc-cross-bronx-reaper.voice: The Reaper speaks tersely when he speaks at all. His teleport allows him to disengage from any losing fight. His raids are calibrated to extract maximum value with minimum exposure.
hooks: the player's caravan is targeted
a Catskill Throne errand requires anti-Reaper action
a Reaper survivor offers intelligence

specific_abilities: T3 Perceptual primary (memory + cross-referencing at scale)

knowledge.detail: the library's full catalog (memorized)

npc-pilar-reyes.what_they_want_from_player: retrieval of specific lost or scattered documents; quiet research on specific historical questions
npc-pilar-reyes.voice: Pilar speaks softly and at length when on a topic she cares about. She references documents by call-number and phrase. She is one of the most useful people in the region for anyone needing to know anything from before.
hooks: Pilar commissions a document retrieval
she discovers something dangerous in the archive
she requests apprentice protection in transit

specific_abilities: T4 Perceptual primary (remote-viewing-adjacent + spatial memory); Matter/Energy-T2 fine-drawing precision secondary

knowledge.detail: the most accurate regional geography of any single source
faction territorial movements

npc-mapmaker-trenton.what_they_want_from_player: field verification of specific zones; protection for delivery
npc-mapmaker-trenton.voice: The Mapmaker speaks deliberately. They use technical cartographic language fluently. They are gender-ambiguous in presentation and do not correct misassumption either way.
hooks: the Mapmaker commissions field verification
a faction wants the workshop seized; the Mapmaker requires extraction
a recently revised map shows something disturbing

specific_abilities: T3 Perceptual primary (language and culture intuition)

goals.description: manage Species G heathen-revival research delicately

knowledge.detail: Species G heathen-revival practices in detail
regional language drift specifics
cultural-religious cross-contamination patterns

npc-afolabi.what_they_want_from_player: field interview escort and protection; retrieval of cultural artifacts
npc-afolabi.voice: Afolabi speaks measured English with Yoruba phrases when he forgets himself. He listens more than he talks during fieldwork. He treats every culture he studies with seriousness.
hooks: Afolabi commissions a field interview escort
he requests retrieval of a cultural artifact
he warns of an emerging cultural pattern that disturbs him

specific_abilities: T4 Matter/Energy primary (electrical systems); Perceptual-T4 understanding-mechanisms secondary

npc-mechanic.what_they_want_from_player: retrieval of specific electrical components; protection for apprentices in transit
npc-mechanic.voice: The Mechanic speaks plainly. When discussing technical matters he becomes precise and animated. He treats apprentices like family. Yonkers depends on his work and he knows it.
hooks: the Mechanic commissions component retrieval
a Bear-House handshake has failed
an apprentice has gone missing

specific_abilities: T6 Matter/Energy primary (electromagnetic precision); Perceptual-T3 machine-sympathy secondary

npc-leo-sparks.role: first major post-Onset-manifestation among Bourse merchant families; Mariam's son

goals.description: establish his place in House Okonkwo

his own capability (which he is exploring)

npc-leo-sparks.what_they_want_from_player: mentorship; an excuse to act against his mother's wishes
npc-leo-sparks.voice: Leo speaks like a 19-year-old of his class — articulate, confident, occasionally arrogant, occasionally humble. He is genuinely curious about his own manifestation and willing to discuss it with anyone who treats him as a person rather than as Mariam's son.
hooks: Mariam commissions discreet escort
Leo approaches the player with an independent commission
Leo's manifestation has produced an anomaly requiring assessment

specific_abilities: T3 Biological primary (species-enhanced plant affinity, extraordinary for age)

knowledge.detail: she can sense plant health across her village
her family treats this as ordinary; the village does not

npc-whistling-girl.voice: She speaks as a 9-year-old does. She whistles tunes that make plants respond — she does not yet understand this is unusual. She is a child first and a prodigy second.
hooks: Veldt commissions discreet observation
the village faces a threat and the girl's capability is at risk
a faction emissary has approached the family

specific_abilities: T5 Physical primary; Perceptual-T4 (species-enhanced sight + tactical-awareness) secondary

npc-captain-ngo.role: Fed Continuity district officer; Species I cross-tie

goals.description: protect Species I PG County kin (her own kin)

knowledge.detail: Reeve's anti-Species-I operations in her district

npc-captain-ngo.what_they_want_from_player: intelligence on Reeve's PG operations; protection for Species I kin in her district
npc-captain-ngo.voice: Ngo speaks the same way to her commanders as to her kin elders. She is quietly pious about her professional obligation. She has not been asked to choose between Fed Continuity and Species I. She knows the day is coming.
hooks: Ngo commissions intelligence work
a Reeve operation against Species I requires response
she warns the player of district officer defection

specific_abilities: T6 Temporal/Spatial primary (harbor-crossing permission system); Perceptual-T3 threat-detection secondary

npc-bridgemaster.what_they_want_from_player: delicate harbor errands; quiet succession assessment
npc-bridgemaster.voice: The Bridgemaster speaks little. The harbor work is conducted in coordinated silence. When questioned, the Bridgemaster answers directly and briefly.
hooks: the Bridgemaster commissions a harbor errand
the player witnesses Bridgemaster preparations for succession scenario
Chen suspects Bridgemaster ambition and wants quiet verification

specific_abilities: T9 (cognition operating on scales humans cannot map)

knowledge.detail: the Prior Cycles theology

what the eldritch Sovereigns are (his account differs from human assumptions)

npc-grey-walker.what_they_want_from_player: rarely engages humans; would request specific environmental restoration
npc-grey-walker.voice: Grey Walker speaks human English with effort. The voice is low and resonant. Sentences are short. Conversations include long silences that are not pauses for thought but periods of attention to other things. Time with him moves differently.
hooks: Preston requests envoy contact
the player witnesses inter-Warped coordination
Grey Walker requests a specific human gesture

specific_abilities: T8 (superhuman pattern recognition)

knowledge.detail: Bourse internal politics in detail
which factions exchange what intelligence

npc-old-black.what_they_want_from_player: delivery of specific messages; environmental observations he cannot make himself
npc-old-black.voice: Old Black speaks human English fluently with a slight chittering undertone. He is patient, witty, and considerably smarter than most humans he speaks with. He treats the Bourse arrangement as a long partnership rather than a transaction.
hooks: Old Black commissions a delivery

he warns the player of a Bourse internal matter

knowledge.detail: migration patterns
which CJL townships honor the treaty fully

npc-white-horse-princeton.voice: The White Horse speaks slowly. The voice is unusual but comprehensible. Sentences are short. The being is patient.
hooks: CJL commissions treaty-enforcement

specific_abilities: T8

npc-old-badger.role: formal Lehigh Principalities treaty Warped party

subterranean Carbon County terrain

npc-old-badger.what_they_want_from_player: investigation of the intrusion; boundary verification
npc-old-badger.voice: Old Badger speaks gruffly. Sentences are very short. The being is pragmatic and patient. The Lehigh lords respect him.

specific_abilities: T8 (this is "The Salt-Walker" in Part XXVIII)

npc-salt-judge.role: visits Crabclaw and Brother Tam settlements; tidal forecaster

npc-salt-judge.what_they_want_from_player: delivery of warnings to specific settlements
npc-salt-judge.voice: The Salt-Judge speaks slowly and in heavily textured English. The voice produces strange harmonics. The visits are brief and transactional.

specific_abilities: T7

npc-abel.role: permanent Yonkers Compact resident; advises Three Judges on local disputes

npc-three-judges.current_state: formally integrated with npc-three-judges (Obi's relationship primarily)

knowledge.detail: Compact internal dynamics in detail

npc-abel.what_they_want_from_player: investigation work the Compact cannot do internally
npc-abel.voice: Abel speaks human English in a soft, unhurried voice. He has the quiet humor of a being who has been a dog for years and finds the situation amusing. He is sincerely fond of Obi.
hooks: Abel commissions investigation

a Compact dispute requires Abel's perspective

npc-lost-thing.species: warped (species ambiguous; possibly mound-building rodent line, transformed beyond classification)
npc-lost-thing.role: hostile Warped sovereign in deepest Pine Barrens

goals.description: territorial; respects The Listening but is not aligned with the cult in any way humans understand

knowledge.detail: claims pre-cycle existence (consistent with other T8+ Warped)

npc-lost-thing.what_they_want_from_player: the player should not encounter The Lost Thing
npc-lost-thing.voice: The Lost Thing has not been recorded as speaking to any human survivor. Encounters end. The being is one of the most dangerous individual entities in the mid-Atlantic and one of the least understood.
hooks: investigation of a missing expedition
The Listening references The Lost Thing in a message
a Warped contact warns of The Lost Thing's recent movements
